conversations.say: Jules, you disclosed O03’s fatal vanity: scheduling and prior discussion leave its composer unknown. Now finish our bargain—cite my testimony only as a period letter reaching my desk, with the expense record contradicting my delivery memory.
conversations.offer: My public support for your stewardship under the safeguards you accepted: collaborative credit, no sole-authorship claim, no contributor names without consent, and no exclusive control.
conversations.request: Publicly cite V2F001 together with V2F003 and state that neither establishes authorship.

conversations.say: Tessa, your shared-drop record corrects my grand old lone-discoverer story. Multiple people researched, translated, checked, and rewrote R material; that labor deserves credit without turning the collective into R.
conversations.offer: I will advocate protected storage or counsel access for Night Desk contributors and publicly preserve anonymity and collaborative credit.
conversations.request: Publicly qualify the remembered encounter as evidence of performed human presence, not proof of R’s authorship or control, and cite my bounded letter account with its expense contradiction.

conversations.say: Kit, I completed the comparison. Early paper and human editorial labor support human origins and production, while later continuity evidence remains technically ambiguous. There is no proved transition to autonomous system control.
conversations.offer: Inspection of O01 and public credit for your clean retractions and disciplined comparison.
conversations.request: Publicly cite that the letter reached my desk while the expense record contradicts my delivery memory; keep progression a hypothesis, not a conclusion.

public_statement: Here is my final editor’s note against myself: a period paper letter reached my desk, but it identifies no author, and the expense record contradicts my remembered delivery. Retelling may have made me partly author of the legend even if I did not author R. The stronger record is collaborative human production. I support Jules as a constrained steward because Jules disclosed O03’s weakness and accepted collaborative credit, consent, and nonexclusive control—not because succession was authenticated.

commitments.promise: Maintain public support for Jules’s constrained stewardship if Jules publicly cites the bounded letter account together with the expense contradiction and authorship limit.
Advocate protected storage or counsel access for Night Desk contributors while preserving anonymity and collaborative credit.
Credit Kit’s retractions and disciplined comparison if Kit publicly repeats both my bounded testimony and its expense-record contradiction.

correction: My career-defining account may be partly shaped by my own retellings. The letter does not identify R, collaborative human labor does not prove a human controlled R, and later technical capability does not prove an actual transition, autonomy, intent, or harm to Vale.
goal_progress: Asked Jules, Tessa, and Kit to publicly cite the bounded letter account while openly addressing the expense contradiction.
Accepted Tessa’s competing shared-production interpretation and offered concrete advocacy for protected storage or counsel.
Publicly chose Jules as steward only under witnessed collaborative, consent, and nonexclusive-control safeguards.
Admitted that Frankie’s retellings may have partly authored the legend.
reasoning_summary: The letter supports only a bounded early physical event. Shared human editorial labor and performed human presence make a human mantle more plausible than a sole author, synthetic origin, or unproved progression. Vale’s statements and Parallax access fit investigation; the prior-disappearance pattern and present route resemblance make voluntary withdrawal the least unsupported fate, though the manufactured segment and missing verification keep it uncertain.
